rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the root cause to invalid feature or helper declarations and fix or remove the offending clauses.
For implementation-level contracts, use assign only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables; avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers and declared helpers within the same annex scope.
Type mismatches in AGREE expressions should be resolved by aligning helper declarations with their usage types.
Package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code must match configured AADL dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Use only valid AADL feature names and declared constants/variables in AGREE expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use component type annexes for such contracts.
